rulings.Case Title: MARK MIGDAL et al VS. MASHA LEVINSON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Order Compelling Deft'S Responses To Form Interrogatories And Special Interrogatories, That Req.'S For Admission Be Deemed Admitted And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, July 16, 2012, Line 3, PLAINTIFF MARK MIGDAL, MARK MIGDAL AS TRUSTEE OF THE MARK MIGDAL 2000 Motion For Order Compelling Defendan'S Responses To Form Interrogatories And Special Interrogatories, That Requests For Admission Be Deemed Admitted And For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Anthony Schoenberg, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion granted. It is hereby ordered that each defendant shall provide full and complete responses without objection to Plaintiff's form interrogatories and set no. one special interrogatories propounded on Defendants Masha Levinson, Leon Taylor, and Leon Taylor as Trustee of The Emily Taylor 1999 Revocable Trust within 10 days of this order. It is hereby ordered that all matters in set no. one of requests for admission on Masha Levinson, set no. one of requests for admission on Leon Taylor, set no. one of requests for admission on Leon Taylor as Trustee of the Emily Taylor 1999 Revocable Trust, and the genuineness of the documents therein be deemed admitted. It is hereby ordered that Defendants or their counsel to pay Plaintiff's attorney monetary sanctions in the amount of $1,110 within 10 days of this order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to TSchoenberg@fbm.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: DOLBY LABORATORIES LICENSING CORPORATION VS. ARCSOFT INC. et al*****CASE TRANSFERRED TO SANTA CLARA COUNTY*****
rulings.Calendar Matter: (Amended) Third Party Deft Notice Of Motion To Sever Under Cal Code Civ Procedure 1048(B) And To Transfer Under Cccp 396b
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, June 16, 2011, Line 13, CROSS DEFENDANT SANDISK CORPORATION'S (Amended) Third Party DefENDANT Notice Of Motion To Sever Under CalIFORNIA Code OF CivIL Procedure 1048(B) And To Transfer Under CCCP 396b. Hearing required. For judicial economy reasons, the court is disinclined to grant severance as requested by SanDisk. However, in light of plaintiff's willingness to have the entire case transferred to Santa Clara County, the court seeks the parties' views on whether that can and/or should be done in the context of this motion given that SanDisk did not request such relief. =(302/HEK)
